title: Immigration Casework and Information - Yasmin Qureshi
meta_description: Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden
text_excerpt: Immigration Casework and Information - Yasmin Qureshi Skip to main content Yasmin Qureshi Labour Member of Parliament for Bolton South and Walkden Join Get Involved Donate Homepage News Articles and Press Parliamentary Activity About Contact Me Useful Contacts Privacy Policy Acceptable Behaviour Policy Help and Advice Immigration Casework and Information More Search Search the website Search Menu Immigration Casework and Information Asking your MP for assistance Please note that Members of Parliament and their staff are not legally permitted to give immigration advice. If you need immigration advice, you should contact a suitably qualified and insured immigration adviser. You can find advisers here: https://portal.oisc.gov.uk/s/adviser-finder Please could you provide all of the details below in order for Ms. Qureshi to contact the Home Office regarding your immigration matter: Full Name Full Address with Post Code Date of Birth Application Type Application Reference Number/ Home Office reference number Date that the application was submitted Please could you also go into your online account and take a screenshot of the page showing the application reference number and the date the application was made, and then send that screenshot to us. Asking your MP to chase up the progress on an application Unless your application is very urgent, please do not ask the MP to chase up an application which is still within the service standards periods for the relevant type of application: Visa processing times for applications made inside the UK Visa processing times for application made outside the UK Please note that applications for Further Leave to Remain on the Basis of Family and Private Life (FLTR-FP) do not have a service standard. Please do not ask the MP to chase any FLTR-FP applications unless your circumstances have become urgent. Consent to Act We will not be able to take any action without the signed consent of the applicant. Therefore, we will require a ‘wet signature’ signed consent form from the applicant – i.e. signed with a pen, not just typed. The applicant should complete and sign this form: UKVI – Consent for the MP to Act . Please return the form to qureshiy2@parliament.uk as soon as possible A scan or photograph of the complete form will be acceptable. Once we have received this information and consent form, we will look into your issue. Self-Help Right to Remain is an organisation which provides lots of guidance and assistance for those applying for the right to remain in the UK. have an excellent free-to-use Toolkit which provide a step-by-step guide to the asylum and immigration system, helping applicants to understand their and navigate the system. The information provided comes not only legal professionals but also from people have been through the system themselves and understand the difficulties and emotional aspects of the process. Migrant Help provides independent advice and guidance to assist people seeking asylum in the UK, including applying for accommodation and financial support. They may also provide specialist support and accommodation to victims of human trafficking and modern slavery, and their dependants. The Home Office public enquiries email address has now been discontinued. If you want to contact the Home Office directly, please go here: https://www.gov.uk/guidance/contact-the-home-office Keeping your details up-to-date with the Home Office Visa Applicants It is essential that you notify the Home Office of any changes in your details, including: passport/travel documents home address or postal address phone number email address change of legal representative Failure to do so could lead to delays in processing your application. Your MP cannot update your details for you; you must do this yourself by going to this web page: https://visa-address-update.service.gov.uk/ Asylum Seekers If you are an asylum seeker, please email your updated details to asylumcentralcommunicationshub@homeoffice.gov.uk As
